title: TAL Paracellular Mg2+ Reabsorption
buzzwords: Major nephron site for cation reabsorption
Handles ~70% of filtered divalent cation
Driven by lumen-positive transepithelial voltage
Passive movement between cells, not through them
Voltage generated by NKCC2 plus ROMK recycling
Loop diuretics and CaSR activation abolish it
Tight-junction pore protein mutations cause wasting
Claudin-16 and claudin-19 paracellular pathway

title: DCT Transcellular Mg2+ Reabsorption (TRPM6)
buzzwords: Fine-tunes final urinary divalent cation excretion
Active transcellular route handling ~10% of filtered load
Apical entry through a melastatin-family cation channel
Basolateral exit via NCX and PMCA
Stimulated by EGF and parathyroid hormone
Cetuximab impairs it by blocking EGF receptor
Gitelman's defective NCC indirectly reduces its activity
Apical TRPM6 channel in distal convoluted tubule

title: Hypomagnesemia: Causes and Management
buzzwords: Muscle cramps, tetany, and seizures
Prolonged QT interval with risk of arrhythmia
FEMg >3-4% indicates renal wasting
PPIs and chronic diarrhea cause GI losses
Loop and thiazide diuretics drive urinary loss
Cisplatin, aminoglycosides, amphotericin B nephrotoxins
Refractory wasting responds to amiloride or SGLT2 inhibitor
Torsades de pointes treated with IV magnesium sulfate

title: Hypermagnesemia
buzzwords: Rare electrolyte disturbance, usually iatrogenic
Symptoms only at levels above 5 mg/dL
Muscle weakness, lethargy, and hypotension
ECG mimics hyperkalemia with peaked T waves
Loss of deep tendon reflexes is hallmark
Dialysis reserved for anuric patients
Advanced CKD patient given excess oral supplement
Preeclampsia patient on continuous MgSO4 infusion

title: Refractory Hypokalemia and Hypocalcemia from Mg2+ Deficiency
buzzwords: Low K+ that won't correct with replacement
Low Ca2+ that won't correct with replacement
Both deficiencies coexist and resist therapy
Underlying cation deficit must be repleted first
Impaired PTH secretion and end-organ resistance
Excessive distal K+ secretion from disinhibited ROMK
Loss of intracellular Mg2+ block on K+ channel
Parathyroid CaSR fails without adequate magnesium
